memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Helsby, Hellsby, Helsbie, Helsbee, Hellsbee and many more. First found in Cheshire where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor of Helmsby. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Richard Hellsby who landed in North America in 1710. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: In God is my hope. Recorded in the spellings of Helsby, Helsbie, and the dialectals Elsby, Elsbie, Elsbury, and possibly others, this is an English surname. It is locational and is probably from the village name of Helsby in the county of Cheshire. It may also be topographical from residence by either a cave near a farm from the Scandanavian 'hellr' and '-bi', a farm, or perhaps from pre-7th century Old English 'aldr-burh' meaning a hill covered with alder trees. Locational surnames names were "from" names. That is to say names that were usually given to people after they moved to another area, the easiest way to identify a stranger being to call him, or sometimes her, by the name of the place from whence they came. Spelling being at best erratic and local dialects very thick, lead to the development of alternative spellings, some far removed from the original. The first recorded spelling of the family name may be that of Agnes Helsby at the church of St Michael Bassishaw in the city of London on 29 May 1580, and later that of Henry Elsby at St James Clerkenwelll, on 20 October 1620 when he married Katherine Pellam. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hellesby於1268年出生在英國爾桑頓 馬福時，他的父親Alan de Acton of Hellesby18歲，母親Beatrix Hatton10歲。他於1293年與Alice Hawise de Trussell在英國英格蘭爾桑頓 馬福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭越可爾。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hellesby was born in 1268, in Thornton Mayow, Cheshire, England, his father, Alan de Acton of Hellesby, was 18 and his mother, Beatrix Hatton, was 10. He married Alice Hawise de Trussell in 1293, in Thornton Mayow, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: William Hellesby(이)가 1268년 영국 체실르 토른턴 메이요에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan de Acton of Hellesby(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix Hatton(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1293년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 토른턴 메이요에서 Alice Hawise de Trussell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 사망했다.
